items.title: OpenAI introduces GPT-4.2 Turbo for reasoning
items.summary: OpenAI announced GPT-4.2 Turbo with improved chain-of-thought control, longer context windows, and faster function calling for enterprise-scale agents.

items.title: New safety evals for multimodal models
items.summary: A new open framework benchmarks hallucination, content safety, and robustness for text+image+audio models across multiple scenarios.

items.title: Gemini Code Assist updates: better context and refactors
items.summary: New features include workspace-wide context, safer automated refactors, and tighter IDE integrations for JavaScript, Python, and Java.

items.title: Scaling reinforcement learning for real-world robotics
items.summary: Researchers describe a pipeline that blends offline datasets with online fine-tuning, improving sample efficiency for manipulation tasks.

items.title: AlphaFold 3.0: an early look at multi-protein complexes
items.summary: A preview of modeling protein–protein interactions and post-translational modifications, with new benchmarks for biological accuracy.

items.title: Game theory meets LLMs: negotiating with reasoning agents
items.summary: Pairing strategic reasoning with language models enables more robust multi-agent negotiation under uncertainty.

items.title: Constitutional AI v2: safer instruction following at scale
items.summary: Anthropic outlines a revised constitutional approach that reduces adversarial prompt exploits while preserving helpfulness.

items.title: Datasets 3.0 release: streaming transforms and lakehouse sync
items.summary: The new Datasets version adds zero-copy streaming transforms, improved dataset cards, and connectors for Delta/Apache Iceberg.

items.title: Mistral raises Series B to expand open-source model roadmap
items.summary: The French AI startup secured new funding to accelerate research on mid-size LLMs and inference-optimized training.

items.title: Adobe brings generative fill to its mobile photo apps
items.summary: Generative fill and object removal tools arrive on iOS and Android, with privacy controls for on-device processing.

items.title: NVIDIA unveils RTX inference suite for edge deployments
items.summary: A new SDK targets low-latency LLM and vision workloads on consumer and workstation GPUs, with quantization and KV-cache optimizations.

items.title: AI agents move from demos to enterprise workflows
items.summary: Vendors roll out guardrailed task automation for IT support, sales ops, and procurement—still human-in-the-loop.

items.title: Planning with World Models under Partial Observability
items.summary: This paper introduces a model-based RL approach that learns latent dynamics to plan across long horizons with sparse rewards.

items.title: Efficient tokenization for long-context language models
items.summary: Authors present a subword scheme and runtime decoder that reduce memory by 30% while preserving perplexity on long-form datasets.

items.title: TechCrunch: Mistral raises Series B (discussion)
items.summary: Community discussion on Mistral’s funding and its implications for open-source model development.

items.title: Andrej Karpathy on small LLMs and product-centered evals
items.summary: A conversation on why smaller, faster models with strong evals can outperform giant models in real user experiences.

items.title: Rob Reid on biotech, wet lab acceleration, and AI risk
items.summary: An in-depth discussion on dual-use biotechnologies, lab automation, and governance frameworks for risk mitigation.

items.title: D: Open-source VLM beats GPT-4V on document QA
items.summary: A GitHub release claims a lightweight vision-language model surpasses GPT-4V on a document QA benchmark.

items.title: [Guide] Run 13B models on laptops with 16GB RAM
items.summary: A practical guide using GGUF quantization, RoPE scaling, and low-rank adapters to hit usable speeds on consumer CPUs.

items.title: Anthropic debuts Claude-Next with faster tool use
items.summary: Claude-Next focuses on tool-calling reliability and lower latency for common workflows like research and summarization.

items.summary: This article predates the allowed window and should be excluded in processing.

items.title: Future of AGI panel: beyond 2026
items.summary: A panel discussion scheduled in the far future—this item should be excluded as it exceeds the +1 day future limit.
